DevOps Project · Linux · RHEL 10

RHEL Repository
Management

Production-grade offline repository setup on Red Hat Enterprise Linux. Covers ISO mounting, repo file creation, persistent fstab entries, version locking, and enterprise internal repo server architecture.

RHEL 10 DNF fstab nano ISO Mount Version Lock Offline Repo Enterprise Linux
View on GitHub Hire Me
Media Showcase
Project Documentation

❓The Problem

In production Linux environments — especially in air-gapped, banking, or enterprise systems — internet access is restricted or completely unavailable. Without proper repository configuration, package installation fails, breaking automation pipelines and CI/CD workflows. The challenge is to establish a reliable, controlled, and repeatable package management system that works offline.

✅Solution

Configure an offline repository on RHEL 10 using an ISO file as the package source. Mount the ISO, create custom .repo files pointing to local filesystem paths, persist the mount via /etc/fstab, and layer in version locking and priority controls for full production-grade stability.

⚡ Production Goal: Control + Predictability + Security

🔧Why DevOps Needs Repository Knowledge

ReasonWhy It Matters
StabilityPrevents unexpected package updates breaking production
SecurityAvoids downloading unapproved packages from the internet
ComplianceEnsures only audited, approved packages are installed
AutomationCI/CD pipelines require reproducible, controlled installs
TroubleshootingFixes broken package systems without internet dependency

📦Repository Types

Online Repo — Internet-based, requires active subscription. Used in dev/test/cloud systems where network is available.


Offline Repo — Uses ISO file directly. No internet required. Essential for secure, air-gapped production environments.

bash — check repos 
# List all configured repos
harsh@devops:~$ dnf repolist
repo id                                   repo name
rhel-10-for-x86_64-baseos-rpms           Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10 BaseOS
rhel-10-for-x86_64-appstream-rpms        Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10 AppStream

# Install a package (downloads from configured repo)
harsh@devops:~$ dnf install nginx

💿ISO Mounting

Two methods depending on your setup — VM with virtual CD drive, or dual-boot with ISO file on disk.

bash — mount ISO 
# --- VM Method (virtual CD drive) ---
harsh@devops:~$ lsblk
NAME   MAJ:MIN RM  SIZE RO TYPE MOUNTPOINTS
sr0     11:0    1  9.8G  0 rom

harsh@devops:~$ sudo mount /dev/sr0 /mnt/iso
mount: /mnt/iso: WARNING: source write-protected, mounted read-only.

# --- Dual Boot Method (ISO file on disk) ---
harsh@devops:~$ sudo mount -o loop ~/iso/rhel-10.iso /mnt/iso
mount: /mnt/iso: WARNING: source write-protected, mounted read-only.
# -o loop = treats ISO file as a virtual disk

📝Creating the Repo File

All repo configuration files live in /etc/yum.repos.d/. Create one for the offline ISO source.

bash — create repo file 
harsh@devops:~$ sudo vi /etc/yum.repos.d/offline.repo
ini — /etc/yum.repos.d/offline.repo 
[Offline-BaseOS]
name=Offline BaseOS
baseurl=file:///mnt/iso/BaseOS
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[Offline-AppStream]
name=Offline AppStream
baseurl=file:///mnt/iso/AppStream
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
⚠ Important: Must use file:/// — triple slash means "file protocol with no hostname, starting from root of local filesystem."
bash — clean & rebuild cache 
# Remove old cached metadata
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf clean all
21 files removed

# Download fresh repo metadata
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf makecache
Offline BaseOS                                  3.1 MB/s | 3.2 MB     00:01
Offline AppStream                               4.6 MB/s | 7.8 MB     00:01
Metadata cache created.

🗂️Persistent Mount via /etc/fstab

Without fstab configuration, the ISO mount is lost on every reboot. Add a permanent entry so the system auto-mounts on boot.

bash — edit fstab 
harsh@devops:~$ sudo vi /etc/fstab
# Add this line:
fstab — /etc/fstab entry 
/home/harsh/iso/rhel-10.iso  /mnt/iso  iso9660  loop,defaults,nofail  0  0
nofail — Any mount not required by core Linux should include nofail. This allows the system to boot even if the mount has an error. Core mounts like /, /boot, /var, /home should NEVER use nofail.
bash — test fstab entry 
harsh@devops:~$ sudo mount -a
# Run after EVERY new fstab entry
# No output = success. Error = fix your entry before reboot!

🎛️Repo Enable / Disable Control

Temporarily disable all repos and force install from only the offline repo — critical for CI/CD environments where reproducibility is required.

bash — force offline install (temporary) 
# Only for this single command — offline repo only
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf install tree --disablerepo="*" --enablerepo="Offline-*"
Dependencies resolved.
Package   Arch    Version    Repository      Size
tree      x86_64  2.1.0-4    Offline-AppStream  56 k
Installing: 1 Package
Complete!
bash — permanent enable/disable 
# Permanently disable a repo
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf config-manager --set-disabled rhel-10-for-x86_64-appstream-rpms

# Permanently enable a repo
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf config-manager --set-enabled rhel-10-for-x86_64-appstream-rpms
# Internally edits enabled=0/1 in /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo files

📊Repo Priority

When multiple repos provide the same package, priority controls which one wins. Lower number = higher priority.

bash — install priority plugin 
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf install dnf-plugins-core
ini — add priority to repo file 
[Offline-BaseOS]
name=Offline BaseOS
baseurl=file:///mnt/iso/BaseOS
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
priority=1    # Lower = higher priority

🔒Version Locking

Prevents accidental package upgrades in production. Lock a package to its currently installed version, or specify a target version manually.

bash — install versionlock plugin 
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf install python3-dnf-plugin-versionlock
bash — full version lock workflow 
# 1. Check installed version before locking
harsh@devops:~$ dnf list installed nginx
nginx.x86_64   1.24.0-1.el10   @rhel-10-for-x86_64-appstream-rpms

# 2. Lock current version
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf versionlock add nginx
Adding versionlock on: nginx-1.24.0-1.el10.*

# 3. Verify lock
harsh@devops:~$ dnf versionlock list
nginx-1.24.0-1.el10.*

# 4. Remove a lock
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf versionlock delete nginx
Deleting versionlock on: nginx-1.24.0-1.el10.*

# 5. Clear ALL locks
harsh@devops:~$ sudo dnf versionlock clear

# Locks stored at: /etc/dnf/plugins/versionlock.list
harsh@devops:~$ cat /etc/dnf/plugins/versionlock.list
Advanced locking: Lock to major version only with nginx-1.24.* — allows minor updates within 1.24 but blocks 1.25+.

🏢Internal Repo Server (Enterprise)

For large organizations with many servers, set up a central repo server once — all client machines pull packages from it via HTTP. Used in banks, enterprises, and secure air-gapped environments.

bash — internal repo server setup 
# On the Repo Server:
harsh@reposerver:~$ sudo dnf install httpd -y
harsh@reposerver:~$ sudo cp -r /mnt/iso/* /var/www/html/rhel10/
harsh@reposerver:~$ sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=http
harsh@reposerver:~$ sudo firewall-cmd --reload
harsh@reposerver:~$ sudo systemctl enable --now httpd

# On each Client Server — create repo file:
harsh@client01:~$ sudo vi /etc/yum.repos.d/internal.repo
ini — client repo pointing to internal server 
[Internal-BaseOS]
name=Internal BaseOS
baseurl=http://192.168.1.100/rhel10/BaseOS
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[Internal-AppStream]
name=Internal AppStream
baseurl=http://192.168.1.100/rhel10/AppStream
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

💡Key Lessons Learned

  • Always run sudo mount -a after every fstab change — a typo will prevent boot.
  • nofail is essential for non-core mounts — never block boot for optional storage.
  • Triple slash in baseurl — file:/// is mandatory for local file protocol.
  • Version lock before production — always check current version first with dnf list installed.
  • dnf clean all → dnf makecache — always rebuild cache after any repo change.
  • Permanent vs temporary — understand when to use --disablerepo (one command) vs config-manager (permanent).
